title.zh: 卷三十一 陳勝項籍傳
title.en: Volume 31: Chen Sheng and Xiang Yu
title.raw: 卷三十一 陳勝項籍傳 Volume 31: Chen Sheng and Xiang Yu

sentences.zh: 陳勝 吳廣

translations.literal: Chen Sheng and Wu Guang.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng and Wu Guang.

sentences.zh: 陳勝字涉，陽城人。

translations.literal: Chen Sheng, style name She, was a man of Yangcheng.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng, courtesy name She, came from Yangcheng.

sentences.zh: 吳廣，字叔，陽夏人也。

translations.literal: Wu Guang, style name Shu, was a man of Yangxia.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Guang, courtesy name Shu, was from Yangxia.

sentences.zh: 勝少時，嘗與人傭耕。

translations.literal: When Sheng was young, he once hired himself out to plow with others.
translations.idiomatic: In his youth, Chen Sheng worked as a hired farm laborer.

sentences.zh: 輟耕之壟上，悵然甚久，曰：「苟富貴，無相忘！」

translations.literal: Resting from plowing on the ridge, he was despondent for a long while and said, "If we become rich and noble, let us not forget one another!"
translations.idiomatic: One day, while taking a break on the field ridge, he sighed for a long time and said, "If one of us ever rises to wealth and rank, we must not forget each other."

sentences.zh: 傭者笑而應曰：「若為傭耕，何富貴也？」

translations.literal: The hired men laughed and replied, "If you are hired to plow, what wealth and nobility could you have?"
translations.idiomatic: The other laborers laughed: "You are a farm hand. How could you ever become rich or noble?"

sentences.zh: 勝太息曰：「嗟乎，燕雀安知鴻鵠之志哉！」

translations.literal: Sheng gave a deep sigh and said, "Alas, how can sparrows know the ambition of swans!"
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng sighed, "How could little sparrows understand the aspirations of a great swan?"

sentences.zh: 勝、廣素愛人，士卒多為用。

translations.literal: Sheng and Guang had long treated people kindly, and many soldiers were willing to serve them.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng and Wu Guang were known for treating others well, so many soldiers followed them readily.

sentences.zh: 將尉醉，廣故數言欲亡，忿尉，令辱之，以激怒其眾。

translations.literal: The commandant was drunk; Guang deliberately spoke repeatedly of fleeing, angering the commandant and making him humiliate Guang, in order to stir the troops' rage.
translations.idiomatic: When the commandant got drunk, Wu Guang kept talking as if he meant to desert, provoking him into public abuse and whipping so the troops would become enraged.

sentences.zh: 尉果笞廣。

translations.literal: The commandant indeed flogged Guang.
translations.idiomatic: The commandant did whip Wu Guang.

sentences.zh: 尉劍挺，廣起奪而殺尉。

translations.literal: The commandant drew his sword; Guang rose, seized it, and killed him.
translations.idiomatic: When the commandant drew his sword, Wu Guang sprang up, took it from him, and killed him.

sentences.zh: 勝佐之，并殺兩尉。

translations.literal: Sheng assisted him and together they killed the two commandants.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng joined in, and together they killed both officers.

sentences.zh: 召令徒屬曰：「公等遇雨，皆已失期，當斬。

translations.literal: They summoned and ordered the convicts, saying: "You have all encountered rain and already missed the deadline; the penalty is execution.
translations.idiomatic: They gathered the penal recruits and said, "You have all been delayed by rain and missed your reporting date. The sentence is death.

sentences.zh: 藉弟令毋斬，而戍死者固什六七。

translations.literal: Even if by chance you were not executed, those sent to garrison duty die six or seven out of ten.
translations.idiomatic: Even if you escaped immediate execution, six or seven out of ten men sent to frontier garrison die anyway.

sentences.zh: 且壯士不死則已，死則舉大名耳。

translations.literal: Moreover, if a strong man is not to die, so be it; if he dies, he should make a great name.
translations.idiomatic: And if a true man must die, then he should die making a name that will be remembered.

sentences.zh: 侯王將相，寧有種乎！」

translations.literal: Are kings, marquises, generals, and ministers born to their stations?"
translations.idiomatic: Do kings and lords, generals and ministers, come from some separate breed?"

sentences.zh: 徒屬皆曰：「敬受令。」

translations.literal: All the followers said: "We respectfully accept your command."
translations.idiomatic: The men replied, "We respectfully obey your order."

sentences.zh: 乃詐稱公子扶蘇、項燕，從民望也。

translations.literal: Then they falsely claimed to be acting for Prince Fusu and Xiang Yan, following popular hopes.
translations.idiomatic: They then proclaimed, falsely, that they were acting in the names of Prince Fusu and Xiang Yan, because that was what the people wanted.

sentences.zh: 袒右，稱大楚。

translations.literal: They bared their right shoulders and proclaimed Great Chu.
translations.idiomatic: They bared their right shoulders in revolt and declared for Great Chu.

sentences.zh: 為壇而盟，祭以尉首。

translations.literal: They built an altar and swore an oath, sacrificing with the commandants' heads.
translations.idiomatic: They raised an altar, swore a covenant, and offered the slain officers' heads in sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 勝自立為將軍，廣為都尉。

translations.literal: Sheng set himself up as general, and Guang became commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng made himself general and appointed Wu Guang as chief commandant.

sentences.zh: 攻大澤鄉，拔之。

translations.literal: They attacked Daze Township and took it.
translations.idiomatic: They attacked Daze Township and captured it.

sentences.zh: 收兵而攻鄿，蘄下。

translations.literal: Gathering troops, they attacked Qi; Qi fell.
translations.idiomatic: After gathering more troops, they attacked Qi and took it.

sentences.zh: 乃令符離人葛嬰將兵徇蘄以東，攻銍、酇、苦、柘、譙，皆下之。

translations.literal: He then ordered Ge Ying of Fuli to lead troops east of Qi, attacking Zhi, Zan, Ku, Zhe, and Qiao, all of which were taken.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent Ge Ying of Fuli eastward from Qi; he captured Zhi, Zan, Ku, Zhe, and Qiao in succession.

sentences.zh: 行收兵，比至陳，兵車六七百乘，騎千餘，卒數萬人。

translations.literal: As they advanced collecting troops, by the time they reached Chen, they had six to seven hundred war chariots, over a thousand cavalry, and tens of thousands of infantry.
translations.idiomatic: As they marched and recruited, by the time they reached Chen they had six to seven hundred chariots, over a thousand horsemen, and many tens of thousands of foot soldiers.

sentences.zh: 攻陳，陳守令皆不在，獨守丞與戰譙門中。

translations.literal: They attacked Chen; Chen's magistrate and commandant were both absent, and only the assistant commandant fought them inside the Qiao Gate.
translations.idiomatic: They attacked Chen. Its top officials were absent, and only the deputy defended the city, fighting at Qiao Gate.

sentences.zh: 不勝，守丞死。

translations.literal: He was defeated, and the assistant commandant died.
translations.idiomatic: He was beaten and killed.

sentences.zh: 乃入據陳。

translations.literal: Then they entered and occupied Chen.
translations.idiomatic: The rebels then entered and held Chen.

sentences.zh: 數日，號召三老豪桀會計事。

translations.literal: After several days, they summoned elders and local heroes to meet and deliberate.
translations.idiomatic: A few days later, they called together village elders and local strongmen to discuss next steps.

sentences.zh: 皆曰：「將軍身被堅執銳，伐無道，誅暴秦，復立楚之社稷，功宜為王。」

translations.literal: All said: "General, you wear armor and wield sharp weapons, strike down the lawless, punish violent Qin, and restore the altars of Chu; your merit makes you fit to be king."
translations.idiomatic: They all said, "You have armored yourself and taken up arms against tyranny, struck down brutal Qin, and restored Chu's state. By merit, you should be king."

sentences.zh: 勝乃立為王，號為張楚。

translations.literal: Sheng therefore established himself as king, with the title Zhangchu.
translations.idiomatic: So Chen Sheng declared himself king and named his regime Zhangchu.

sentences.zh: 於是諸郡縣苦秦吏暴，皆殺其長吏，將以應勝。

translations.literal: Thereupon all commanderies and counties suffering under Qin officials' cruelty killed their chief officials and prepared to respond to Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: At this, counties and commanderies oppressed by Qin officials rose up, killed their superiors, and moved to join Chen Sheng.

sentences.zh: 乃以廣為假王，監諸將以西擊滎陽。

translations.literal: He then made Guang acting king, supervising the generals to the west in attacking Xingyang.
translations.idiomatic: He appointed Wu Guang acting king and sent him west to oversee the attack on Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 令陳人武臣、張耳、陳餘徇趙，汝陰人鄧宗徇九江郡。

translations.literal: He ordered Wuchen, Zhang Er, and Chen Yu of Chen to campaign in Zhao, and Deng Zong of Ruyin to campaign in Jiujiang Commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched Chen natives Wuchen, Zhang Er, and Chen Yu to rally Zhao, and Deng Zong of Ruyin to rally Jiujiang.

sentences.zh: 當此時，楚兵數千人為聚者不可勝數。

translations.literal: At this time, Chu forces gathering in bands of several thousand were beyond counting.
translations.idiomatic: By then, rebel Chu forces were springing up everywhere in groups of several thousand, too many to count.

sentences.zh: 葛嬰至東城，立襄彊為楚王。

translations.literal: Ge Ying arrived at Dongcheng and set up Xiang Qiang as king of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When Ge Ying reached Dongcheng, he installed Xiang Qiang as king of Chu.

sentences.zh: 後聞勝已立，因殺襄彊，還報。

translations.literal: Later, hearing Sheng had already become king, he therefore killed Xiang Qiang and returned to report.
translations.idiomatic: Later, learning Chen Sheng had already declared kingship, Ge Ying killed Xiang Qiang and returned to report.

sentences.zh: 至陳，勝殺嬰，令魏人周市北徇魏地。

translations.literal: Upon reaching Chen, Sheng killed Ying and ordered Zhou Shi of Wei to campaign north through Wei lands.
translations.idiomatic: When Ge Ying returned to Chen, Chen Sheng executed him and sent Wei native Zhou Shi north to secure Wei territory.

sentences.zh: 廣圍滎陽。

translations.literal: Guang besieged Xingyang.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Guang laid siege to Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 李由為三川守守滎陽，廣不能下。

translations.literal: Li You, commandery governor of Sanchuan, defended Xingyang, and Guang could not take it.
translations.idiomatic: Li You, governor of Sanchuan, held Xingyang, and Wu Guang could not capture it.

sentences.zh: 勝徵國之豪桀與計，以上蔡人房君蔡賜為上柱國。

translations.literal: Sheng summoned the realm's heroes to plan and made Cai Ci of Shangcai, Lord Fang, Supreme Pillar of State.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng called in notable men for strategy and appointed Cai Ci of Shangcai, Lord Fang, as Supreme Pillar of State.

sentences.zh: 周文，陳賢人也，嘗為項燕軍視日，事春申君，自言習兵。

translations.literal: Zhou Wen was a worthy man of Chen. He had once served in Xiang Yan's army as an observer of days, served Lord Chunshen, and claimed to be versed in warfare.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Wen, a respected man from Chen, had served in Xiang Yan's army and at Lord Chunshen's court, and claimed military expertise.

sentences.zh: 勝與之將軍印，西擊秦。

translations.literal: Sheng gave him the general's seal to attack Qin in the west.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng gave him the general's seal and sent him west against Qin.

sentences.zh: 行收兵至關，車千乘，卒十萬，至戲，軍焉。

translations.literal: Advancing while gathering troops, he reached the passes with a thousand chariots and one hundred thousand soldiers; reaching Xi, he camped there.
translations.idiomatic: Recruiting as he advanced, Zhou Wen reached the passes with a thousand chariots and one hundred thousand men, then encamped at Xi.

sentences.zh: 秦令少府章邯免驪山徒、人奴產子，悉發以擊楚軍，大敗之。

translations.literal: Qin ordered Shaofu Zhang Han to pardon Lishan convicts and enslaved women who had borne children, mobilizing them all to strike the Chu army and greatly defeat it.
translations.idiomatic: Qin appointed Zhang Han of the Lesser Treasury, amnestied Lishan convicts and bondservants who had borne children, and threw them into battle; they crushed the Chu army.

sentences.zh: 周文走出關，止屯曹陽。

translations.literal: Zhou Wen fled out of the passes and halted at Caoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Wen retreated out of the passes and stopped at Caoyang.

sentences.zh: 二月餘，章邯追敗之，復走黽池。

translations.literal: After more than two months, Zhang Han pursued and defeated him; he fled again to Mianchi.
translations.idiomatic: After over two months, Zhang Han caught and beat him again, forcing another retreat to Mianchi.

sentences.zh: 十餘日，章邯擊，大破之。

translations.literal: After more than ten days, Zhang Han attacked and utterly defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Ten-odd days later, Zhang Han attacked once more and routed him completely.

sentences.zh: 周文自剄，軍遂不戰。

translations.literal: Zhou Wen cut his own throat, and the army then ceased fighting.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Wen killed himself, and his army collapsed.

sentences.zh: 武臣至邯鄲，自立為趙王，陳餘為大將軍，張耳、召騷為左右丞相。

translations.literal: When Wuchen reached Handan, he set himself up as king of Zhao; Chen Yu became grand general, and Zhang Er and Shao Sao became left and right chancellors.
translations.idiomatic: At Handan, Wuchen declared himself king of Zhao. Chen Yu became grand general, with Zhang Er and Shao Sao as left and right chancellors.

sentences.zh: 勝怒，捕繫武臣等家室，欲誅之。

translations.literal: Sheng was enraged, arrested and imprisoned the families of Wuchen and the others, and wished to execute them.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng was furious and seized the families of Wuchen and his associates, intending to execute them.

sentences.zh: 柱國曰：「秦未亡而誅趙王將相家屬，此生一秦，不如因立之。」

translations.literal: A pillar of state said: "Qin is not yet destroyed, yet you execute the families of Zhao's king and ministers; this would create another Qin. Better to accept and establish them."
translations.idiomatic: A senior minister warned, "Qin is not yet dead. If you kill the families of Zhao's king and ministers, you will only create a second Qin. Better to recognize them."

sentences.zh: 勝乃遣使者賀趙，而徙繫武臣等家屬宮中。

translations.literal: Sheng then sent envoys to congratulate Zhao and moved the imprisoned families of Wuchen and others into the palace.
translations.idiomatic: So Chen Sheng sent envoys to congratulate Zhao and moved those detained families into palace custody.

sentences.zh: 而封張耳子敖為成都君，趣趙兵亟入關。

translations.literal: He also enfeoffed Zhang Er's son Ao as Lord of Chengdu and urged Zhao troops to enter the passes quickly.
translations.idiomatic: He also enfeoffed Zhang Er's son Ao as Lord of Chengdu and pressed Zhao to send troops quickly through the passes.

sentences.zh: 趙王將相相與謀曰：「王王趙，非楚意也。

translations.literal: The Zhao king and his generals and ministers discussed together, saying: "Our king's kingship of Zhao is not Chu's intention.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao's king and ministers conferred: "Our founding of a Zhao kingship was never Chu's intent.

sentences.zh: 楚已誅秦，必加兵於趙。

translations.literal: Once Chu has destroyed Qin, it will surely send troops against Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: After Chu defeats Qin, it will certainly turn its armies on Zhao.

sentences.zh: 計莫如毋西兵，使使北徇燕地以自廣。

translations.literal: The best plan is not to send troops west, but to send forces north to campaign in Yan lands and expand ourselves.
translations.idiomatic: Our best course is to avoid sending troops west; instead we should campaign north in Yan and enlarge our base.

sentences.zh: 趙南據大河，北有燕代，楚雖勝秦，不敢制趙，若不勝秦，必重趙。

translations.literal: With Zhao holding the Yellow River to the south and having Yan and Dai to the north, even if Chu defeats Qin it will not dare control Zhao; if it does not defeat Qin, it will need Zhao all the more.
translations.idiomatic: If Zhao controls the Yellow River in the south and has Yan and Dai in the north, then even a victorious Chu will hesitate to dominate us; if Chu fails against Qin, it will need us even more.

sentences.zh: 趙承秦楚之敝，可以得志於天下。」

translations.literal: Zhao can inherit the exhaustion of Qin and Chu and thereby realize its aims under Heaven."
translations.idiomatic: With Qin and Chu both worn down, Zhao can seize its chance to prevail in the empire."

sentences.zh: 趙王以為然，因不西兵，而遣故上谷卒史韓廣將兵北徇燕。

translations.literal: The Zhao king thought this right; thus he did not send troops west, but dispatched former Shanggu military clerk Han Guang to lead troops north to campaign in Yan.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhao king agreed, withheld western troops, and sent former Shanggu military clerk Han Guang north to rally Yan.

sentences.zh: 燕地貴人豪桀謂韓廣曰：「楚趙皆已立王。

translations.literal: The nobles and local heroes of Yan said to Han Guang: "Chu and Zhao have both established kings.
translations.idiomatic: Yan nobles and strongmen said to Han Guang, "Chu and Zhao have each set up kings.

sentences.zh: 燕雖小，亦萬乘之國也，願將軍立為王。」

translations.literal: Though Yan is small, it too is a state of ten thousand chariots. We wish the general to establish himself as king."
translations.idiomatic: Though Yan is small, it is still a major realm. We ask you, General, to make yourself king."

sentences.zh: 韓廣曰：「廣母在趙，不可。」

translations.literal: Han Guang said: "My mother is in Zhao; I cannot."
translations.idiomatic: Han Guang said, "My mother is in Zhao. I cannot do that."

sentences.zh: 燕人曰：「趙方西憂秦，南憂楚，其力不能禁我。

translations.literal: The people of Yan said: "Zhao now worries west about Qin and south about Chu; its strength cannot restrain us.
translations.idiomatic: The Yan men replied, "Zhao is preoccupied, fearing Qin in the west and Chu in the south. It has no strength to stop us.

sentences.zh: 且以楚之強，不敢害趙王將相之家，今趙又安敢害將軍之家乎？」

translations.literal: Moreover, with Chu's strength, it still dares not harm the families of Zhao's king and ministers. How then would Zhao dare harm your family?"
translations.idiomatic: Besides, even mighty Chu dared not harm the families of Zhao's rulers and ministers. Why would Zhao dare harm yours?"

sentences.zh: 韓廣以為然，乃自立為燕王。

translations.literal: Han Guang thought this right and then set himself up as king of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Han Guang accepted this argument and declared himself king of Yan.

sentences.zh: 居數月，趙奉燕王母家屬歸之。

translations.literal: After several months, Zhao sent back the Yan king's mother and family to him.
translations.idiomatic: After a few months, Zhao returned Han Guang's mother and relatives to Yan.

sentences.zh: 是時，諸將徇地者不可勝數。

translations.literal: At this time, the generals campaigning for territory were too many to count.
translations.idiomatic: By this point, there were countless commanders seizing territory in every direction.

sentences.zh: 周市北至狄，狄人田儋殺狄令，自立為齊王，反擊周市。

translations.literal: Zhou Shi went north to Di; Tian Dan of Di killed the Di magistrate, established himself king of Qi, and turned to attack Zhou Shi.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Shi advanced north to Di, where Tian Dan killed the local magistrate, proclaimed himself king of Qi, and counterattacked Zhou Shi.

sentences.zh: 市軍散，還至魏地，立魏後故甯陵君咎為魏王。

translations.literal: Shi's army dispersed; he returned to Wei lands and installed Jiu, former Lord Ningling of Wei's royal line, as king of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Shi's army scattered, so he withdrew to Wei territory and set up Jiu, of Wei's former ruling house and known as Lord Ningling, as king of Wei.

sentences.zh: 咎在勝所，不得之魏。

translations.literal: Jiu was with Sheng and could not go to Wei.
translations.idiomatic: But Jiu was still with Chen Sheng and could not return to Wei.

sentences.zh: 魏地已定，欲立周市為王，市不肯。

translations.literal: When Wei lands were settled, they wished to establish Zhou Shi as king, but Shi refused.
translations.idiomatic: After Wei territory was secured, many wanted Zhou Shi to become king, but he refused.

sentences.zh: 使者五反，勝乃立甯陵君為魏王，遣之國。

translations.literal: Envoys went five times before Sheng finally installed Lord Ningling as king of Wei and sent him to his state.
translations.idiomatic: Only after five envoys did Chen Sheng finally appoint Lord Ningling king of Wei and send him to his realm.

sentences.zh: 周市為相。

translations.literal: Zhou Shi became chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Shi became Wei's chancellor.

sentences.zh: 將軍田臧等相與謀曰：「周章軍已破，秦兵且至，我守滎陽城不能下，秦軍至，必大敗。

translations.literal: General Tian Zang and others discussed together, saying: "Zhou Zhang's army has already been broken, Qin troops are about to arrive; we are holding Xingyang city and cannot take it. When Qin troops arrive, we will surely suffer great defeat.
translations.idiomatic: General Tian Zang and others said, "Zhou Zhang's forces are already broken and Qin troops are coming. We still cannot take Xingyang. Once Qin arrives, we will be badly defeated.

sentences.zh: 不如少遺兵，足以守滎陽，悉精兵迎秦軍。

translations.literal: Better to leave a small force, enough to guard Xingyang, and use all elite troops to meet Qin troops.
translations.idiomatic: Better to leave only enough men to contain Xingyang and take our elite troops to meet Qin head-on.

sentences.zh: 今假王驕，不知兵權，不可與計，非誅之，事恐敗。」

translations.literal: Now the acting king is arrogant and does not understand military authority; we cannot plan with him. If we do not execute him, our cause may fail."
translations.idiomatic: The acting king is proud and does not understand command. We cannot deliberate with him. If we do not remove him, this campaign may fail."

sentences.zh: 因相與矯陳王令以誅吳廣，獻其首於勝。

translations.literal: Accordingly they together forged King Chen's order to execute Wu Guang and presented his head to Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: So they forged an order in King Chen's name, killed Wu Guang, and sent his head to Chen Sheng.

sentences.zh: 勝使賜田臧楚令尹印，使為上將。

translations.literal: Sheng had Tian Zang granted the seal of Chu commandant and made him supreme general.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng then granted Tian Zang the seal of Chu commandant-in-chief and appointed him supreme commander.

sentences.zh: 田臧乃使諸將李歸等守滎陽城，自以精兵西迎秦軍於敖倉。

translations.literal: Tian Zang then ordered generals Li Gui and others to guard Xingyang city, while he himself led elite troops west to meet Qin at Aocang.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Zang left Li Gui and others to hold Xingyang, then personally led elite troops west to engage Qin at Aocang.

sentences.zh: 與戰，田臧死，軍破。

translations.literal: In battle, Tian Zang died and the army was broken.
translations.idiomatic: In the fighting Tian Zang was killed and his army collapsed.

sentences.zh: 章邯進擊李歸等滎陽下，破之，李歸死。

translations.literal: Zhang Han advanced and struck Li Gui and others below Xingyang, defeating them; Li Gui died.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han then pushed on to Xingyang, defeated Li Gui's force, and killed Li Gui.

sentences.zh: 陽城人鄧說將兵居郯，章邯別將擊破之，鄧說走陳。

translations.literal: Deng Yue of Yangcheng led troops stationed at Tan; Zhang Han's separate force defeated him, and Deng Yue fled to Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yue of Yangcheng held Tan with his troops, but one of Zhang Han's detachments defeated him and drove him back to Chen.

sentences.zh: 銍人五逢將兵居許，章邯擊破之。

translations.literal: Wu Feng of Zhi led troops stationed at Xu; Zhang Han attacked and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Feng of Zhi held Xu with his troops, and Zhang Han defeated him there.

sentences.zh: 五逢亦走陳。

translations.literal: Wu Feng also fled to Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Feng also fled back to Chen.

sentences.zh: 勝誅鄧說。

translations.literal: Sheng executed Deng Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng executed Deng Yue.

sentences.zh: 勝初立時，淩人秦嘉、銍人董惞、符離人朱雞石、取慮人鄭布、徐人丁疾等皆特起，將兵圍東海守於郯。

translations.literal: When Sheng first established himself, Qin Jia of Ling, Dong Xin of Zhi, Zhu Jishi of Fuli, Zheng Bu of Qulu, Xu man Ding Ji, and others had all risen independently, leading troops to besiege the Donghai governor at Tan.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng first rose, Qin Jia of Ling, Dong Xin of Zhi, Zhu Jishi of Fuli, Zheng Bu of Qulu, Ding Ji of Xu, and others raised forces on their own and besieged the governor of Donghai at Tan.

sentences.zh: 勝聞，乃使武平君畔為將軍，監郯下軍。

translations.literal: When Sheng heard, he sent Lord Wuping Pan as general to supervise the army below Tan.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng heard this, he sent Lord Wuping, Pan, as general to oversee the forces at Tan.

sentences.zh: 秦嘉自立為大司馬，惡屬人，告軍吏曰：「武平君年少，不知兵事，勿聽。」

translations.literal: Qin Jia set himself up as grand marshal; disliking to serve another, he told the officers: "Lord Wuping is young and does not understand military affairs. Do not obey him."
translations.idiomatic: Qin Jia made himself grand marshal and resented being subordinate, so he told the officers, "Lord Wuping is young and knows nothing of war. Do not listen to him."

sentences.zh: 因矯以王命殺武平君畔。

translations.literal: Then he forged a royal order and killed Lord Wuping Pan.
translations.idiomatic: He then forged a royal command and killed Lord Wuping Pan.

sentences.zh: 章邯已破五逢，擊陳，柱國房君死。

translations.literal: Zhang Han had already defeated Wu Feng; attacking Chen, Lord Fang, pillar of state, died.
translations.idiomatic: After defeating Wu Feng, Zhang Han attacked Chen, where Lord Fang, a pillar of state, was killed.

sentences.zh: 章邯又進擊陳西張賀軍。

translations.literal: Zhang Han then advanced again to attack Zhang He's army west of Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han advanced further and struck Zhang He's force west of Chen.

sentences.zh: 勝出臨戰，軍破，張賀死。

translations.literal: Sheng went out to face battle; the army was broken and Zhang He died.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng personally took the field, but his army was routed and Zhang He was killed.

sentences.zh: 臘月，勝之汝陰，還至下城父，其御莊賈殺勝以降秦。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, Sheng went to Ruyin; returning to Xiagchengfu, his charioteer Zhuang Jia killed Sheng and surrendered to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month, Chen Sheng went to Ruyin; on the return to Xiagchengfu, his own charioteer Zhuang Jia killed him and defected to Qin.

sentences.zh: 葬碭，諡曰隱王。

translations.literal: He was buried at Dang and given the posthumous title Hidden King.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng was buried at Dang and posthumously styled the "Hidden King."

sentences.zh: 勝故涓人將軍呂臣為蒼頭軍，起新陽，攻陳下之，殺莊賈，復以陳為楚。

translations.literal: Lü Chen, former palace attendant general under Sheng, commanded the Cangtou troops, rose at Xinyang, attacked and took Chen, killed Zhuang Jia, and again made Chen belong to Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Chen, once a palace attendant-general under Chen Sheng, led the Cangtou troops from Xinyang, retook Chen, killed Zhuang Jia, and restored Chen to Chu control.

sentences.zh: 初，勝令銍人宋留將兵定南陽，入武關。

translations.literal: At first, Sheng ordered Song Liu of Zhi to lead troops to pacify Nanyang and enter Wu Pass.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, Chen Sheng had sent Song Liu of Zhi to pacify Nanyang and push through Wu Pass.

sentences.zh: 留已徇南陽，聞勝死，南陽復為秦。

translations.literal: Song Liu had already campaigned through Nanyang; hearing Sheng was dead, Nanyang returned to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Song Liu had already swept through Nanyang, but when news of Chen Sheng's death arrived, Nanyang reverted to Qin.

sentences.zh: 宋留不能入武關，乃東至新蔡，遇秦軍，宋留以軍降秦。

translations.literal: Song Liu could not enter Wu Pass, so he turned east to Xincai, encountered Qin forces, and surrendered with his army.
translations.idiomatic: Unable to break through Wu Pass, Song Liu turned east to Xincai, met Qin troops, and surrendered with his army.

sentences.zh: 秦傳留至咸陽，車裂留以徇。

translations.literal: Qin conveyed Song Liu to Xianyang and tore him apart by chariots as a warning.
translations.idiomatic: Qin sent Song Liu to Xianyang and executed him by dismemberment with chariots as a public warning.

sentences.zh: 秦嘉等聞勝軍敗，乃立景駒為楚王，引兵之方與，欲擊秦軍濟陰下。

translations.literal: When Qin Jia and others heard Sheng's army was defeated, they set up Jing Ju as king of Chu, led troops to Fangyu, and wished to strike the Qin army below Jiyin.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin Jia heard Chen Sheng's forces had collapsed, he and his allies installed Jing Ju as Chu king, moved to Fangyu, and prepared to attack Qin near Jiyin.

sentences.zh: 使公孫慶使齊王，欲與并力俱進。

translations.literal: They sent Gongsun Qing as envoy to the king of Qi, wanting to join strength and advance together.
translations.idiomatic: They sent Gongsun Qing to Qi, seeking a joint advance with combined forces.

sentences.zh: 齊王曰：「陳王戰敗，未知其死生，楚安得不請而立王？」

translations.literal: The king of Qi said: "King Chen has been defeated in battle, and his life or death is unknown. How could Chu set up a king without asking?"
translations.idiomatic: The king of Qi said, "King Chen has been beaten and may already be dead. How can Chu enthrone a king without consultation?"

sentences.zh: 公孫慶曰：「齊不請楚而立王，楚何故請齊而立王？

translations.literal: Gongsun Qing said: "Qi set up a king without asking Chu; why should Chu ask Qi to set up a king?
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Qing replied, "Qi made its own king without asking Chu. Why should Chu ask Qi before doing the same?

sentences.zh: 且楚首事，當令於天下。」

translations.literal: Moreover, Chu was first to rise and should issue commands under Heaven."
translations.idiomatic: And besides, Chu was first to rise; it should lead the realm."

sentences.zh: 田儋殺公孫慶。

translations.literal: Tian Dan killed Gongsun Qing.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Dan then killed Gongsun Qing.

sentences.zh: 秦左右校復攻陳，下之。

translations.literal: Qin left and right colonels again attacked Chen and took it.
translations.idiomatic: Qin's left and right field commanders attacked Chen again and captured it.

sentences.zh: 呂將軍走，徼兵復聚，與番盜英布相遇，攻擊秦左右校，破之青波，復以陳為楚。

translations.literal: General Lü fled; gathering scattered troops again, he met Ying Bu, a bandit of Fan, attacked the Qin left and right colonels, defeated them at Qingbo, and restored Chen to Chu.
translations.idiomatic: General Lu retreated, regrouped stragglers, joined with Ying Bu of Fan, beat the Qin commanders at Qingbo, and brought Chen back under Chu.

sentences.zh: 會項梁立懷王孫心為楚王。

translations.literal: At this time Xiang Liang established Xin, grandson of King Huai, as king of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Around this time, Xiang Liang enthroned Xin, grandson of King Huai, as Chu king.

sentences.zh: 陳勝王凡六月。

translations.literal: Chen Sheng reigned as king for a total of six months.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng's kingship lasted only six months.

sentences.zh: 初為王，其故人嘗與傭耕者聞之，乃之陳，叩宮門曰：「吾欲見涉。」

translations.literal: When he first became king, an old acquaintance who had once hired out to plow with him heard of it, went to Chen, and knocked at the palace gate saying: "I want to see She."
translations.idiomatic: After he became king, a former fellow farm laborer came to Chen and knocked at the palace gate, saying, "I want to see She."

sentences.zh: 宮門令欲縛之。

translations.literal: The gate commandant wanted to bind him.
translations.idiomatic: The gate officer tried to have him seized and tied up.

sentences.zh: 自辯數，乃置，不肯為通。

translations.literal: He repeatedly argued for himself, and was let go, but they would not announce him.
translations.idiomatic: He argued his case again and again, and though they released him, they still refused to announce him.

sentences.zh: 勝出，遮道而呼涉。

translations.literal: When Sheng came out, he blocked the road and called out "She!"
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng came out, the man blocked the road and shouted, "She!"

sentences.zh: 乃召見，載與歸。

translations.literal: Sheng then summoned him for an audience and rode back with him.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng had him brought in and took him back with the procession.

sentences.zh: 入宮，見殿屋帷帳，客曰：「夥，涉之為王沈沈者！」

translations.literal: Entering the palace and seeing halls, chambers, and curtains, the guest said: "Wow, She has become king in grand style!"
translations.idiomatic: Inside the palace, seeing the halls and draperies, the guest exclaimed, "So this is how grand She has become as king!"

sentences.zh: 楚人謂多為夥，故天下傳之，「夥涉為王」，由陳涉始。

translations.literal: The Chu people called "many" huo, so the world transmitted it as "Huo, She became king," and this began with Chen She.
translations.idiomatic: In Chu speech, "huo" meant "many," so people repeated the phrase "Huo, She has become king"; that saying began with Chen She.

sentences.zh: 客出入愈益發舒，言勝故情。

translations.literal: As the guest went in and out, he grew ever more unrestrained and spoke of Sheng's old affairs.
translations.idiomatic: As he came and went freely, the man grew increasingly loose-tongued and kept recounting Chen Sheng's old days.

sentences.zh: 或言「客愚無知，專妄言，輕威。」

translations.literal: Some said, "This guest is foolish and ignorant, speaking reckless nonsense and slighting royal authority."
translations.idiomatic: Some courtiers said, "This man is ignorant and babbles wildly. He undermines royal dignity."

sentences.zh: 勝斬之。

translations.literal: Sheng executed him.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng had him executed.

sentences.zh: 諸故人皆自引去，由是無親勝者。

translations.literal: All old acquaintances withdrew themselves, and because of this none were close to Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: After that, all his old friends distanced themselves, and no one truly drew close to him.

sentences.zh: 以朱防為中正，胡武為司過，主司群臣。

translations.literal: He made Zhu Fang chief censor and Hu Wu director of faults, in charge of supervising all officials.
translations.idiomatic: He appointed Zhu Fang as rectifier and Hu Wu as inspector of faults, placing them over the officials.

sentences.zh: 諸將徇地，至，令之不是者，繫而罪之。

translations.literal: When various generals returned from campaigning, if their orders did not conform, he bound them and punished them.
translations.idiomatic: When field generals came back, if their reports displeased him he had them arrested and punished.

sentences.zh: 以苛察為忠。

translations.literal: He took harsh scrutiny as loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: He treated severity and suspicion as proof of loyalty.

sentences.zh: 其所不善者，不下吏，輒自治。

translations.literal: For those he disliked, he did not send them down to judicial officers, but directly dealt with them himself.
translations.idiomatic: If he disliked someone, he bypassed normal officials and punished the person himself.

sentences.zh: 勝信用之，諸將以故不親附。

translations.literal: Sheng trusted these practices, and for this reason the generals were not close or attached.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng trusted this style of rule, so his generals no longer felt loyal to him.

sentences.zh: 此其所以敗也。

translations.literal: This was the reason he was defeated.
translations.idiomatic: That is why his regime failed.

sentences.zh: 勝雖已死，其所置遣侯王將相竟亡秦。

translations.literal: Although Sheng had already died, the marquis-kings, generals, and ministers he had set up in the end destroyed Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Though Chen Sheng died, the kings, commanders, and ministers he had set in motion ultimately brought Qin down.

sentences.zh: 高祖時為勝置守冢于碭，至今血食。

translations.literal: In Gaozu's time, a tomb guardian was established for Sheng at Dang, and to this day blood sacrifices are offered.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Gaozu, a tomb keeper was assigned to Chen Sheng at Dang, and sacrificial offerings continued there.

sentences.zh: 王莽敗，乃絕。

translations.literal: After Wang Mang's defeat, this then ceased.
translations.idiomatic: Those offerings ended only after Wang Mang fell.

sentences.zh: 項籍字羽，下相人也。

translations.literal: Xiang Ji, style name Yu, was a man of Xiaxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Ji, courtesy name Yu, came from Xiaxiang.

sentences.zh: 初起，年二十四。

translations.literal: When he first rose, he was twenty-four years old.
translations.idiomatic: He was twenty-four when he first rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 其季父梁，梁父即楚名將項燕者也。

translations.literal: His younger paternal uncle was Liang; Liang's father was precisely Xiang Yan, the famous Chu general.
translations.idiomatic: His uncle was Xiang Liang, son of Xiang Yan, the renowned Chu general.

sentences.zh: 家世楚將，封於項，故姓項氏。

translations.literal: His family had for generations been Chu generals, enfeoffed at Xiang, therefore their surname was Xiang.
translations.idiomatic: His lineage had served as Chu generals for generations and held fief at Xiang, hence the clan name Xiang.

sentences.zh: 籍少時，學書不成，去；

translations.literal: When Ji was young, he studied writing but did not complete it, and left.
translations.idiomatic: As a youth, Xiang Ji tried studying letters but quit before mastering them.

sentences.zh: 學劍又不成，去。

translations.literal: He studied swordsmanship and again did not complete it, and left.
translations.idiomatic: He then learned swordplay, and quit that too.

sentences.zh: 梁怒之。

translations.literal: Liang was angry at him.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang was furious with him.

sentences.zh: 籍曰：「書足記姓名而已。

translations.literal: Ji said: "Writing is enough only to record names.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Ji said, "Writing only teaches one to record names.

sentences.zh: 劍一人敵，不足學，學萬人敵耳。」

translations.literal: Swordsmanship is skill to match one man and is not worth learning; I will learn skill to match ten thousand men."
translations.idiomatic: Swordsmanship beats one man at a time; that is not worth learning. I want the art of facing ten thousand."

sentences.zh: 於是梁奇其意，乃教以兵法。

translations.literal: Thereupon Liang found his intent remarkable and then taught him military methods.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang found this ambition unusual and began teaching him military strategy.

sentences.zh: 籍大喜，略知其意，又不肯竟。

translations.literal: Ji was very pleased and roughly understood its meaning, yet was unwilling to finish the course.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Ji was delighted and grasped the main ideas, but still refused to study through to the end.

sentences.zh: 梁嘗有櫟陽逮，請蘄獄掾曹咎書抵櫟陽獄史司馬欣，以故事皆已。

translations.literal: Liang once had a warrant from Liyang; he asked Cao Jiu, prison clerk of Qi, to write to Sima Xin, prison historian of Liyang, and by old connections everything was settled.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang once faced arrest from Liyang, but with a letter from Qi prison clerk Cao Jiu to Liyang jail official Sima Xin, the matter was quietly smoothed over.

sentences.zh: 梁嘗殺人，與籍避仇吳中。

translations.literal: Liang once killed a man and, with Ji, avoided vengeance in Wuzhong.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Liang killed a man, he and Xiang Ji took refuge in Wuzhong.

sentences.zh: 吳中賢士大夫皆出梁下。

translations.literal: All worthy gentry and officials in Wuzhong were beneath Liang.
translations.idiomatic: In Wuzhong, local elites and officials all deferred to Xiang Liang.

sentences.zh: 每有大繇役及喪，梁常主辦，陰以兵法部勒賓客子弟，以知其能。

translations.literal: Whenever there were major corvee levies or funerals, Liang often managed affairs, and secretly used military methods to drill retainers and young men so as to know their abilities.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever there were major labor levies or funerals, Xiang Liang managed the arrangements and quietly used them to drill young followers in military order and test their talent.

sentences.zh: 秦始皇帝東遊會稽，渡浙江，梁與籍觀。

translations.literal: When the First Emperor of Qin toured east to Kuaiji and crossed the Zhejiang, Liang and Ji watched.
translations.idiomatic: When the First Emperor toured east to Kuaiji and crossed the Zhejiang, Xiang Liang and Xiang Ji stood watching.

sentences.zh: 籍曰：「彼可取而代也。」

translations.literal: Ji said: "He can be taken and replaced."
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Ji said, "That man can be replaced."

sentences.zh: 梁掩其口，曰：「無妄言，族矣！」

translations.literal: Liang covered his mouth and said: "Do not speak wildly; we will be exterminated!"
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang clapped a hand over his mouth: "Do not speak recklessly, or our whole clan will be wiped out!"

sentences.zh: 梁以此奇籍。

translations.literal: Liang therefore found Ji extraordinary.
translations.idiomatic: From this, Xiang Liang knew Xiang Ji was no ordinary man.

sentences.zh: 籍長八尺二寸，力扛鼎，才氣過人。

translations.literal: Ji was eight chi two cun tall, with strength to lift cauldrons; his talent and spirit surpassed others.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Ji was over eight chi tall, strong enough to lift tripods, with gifts and force of character beyond other men.

sentences.zh: 吳中弟子皆憚籍。

translations.literal: All young men of Wuzhong feared Ji.
translations.idiomatic: All the young men of Wuzhong stood in awe of Xiang Ji.

sentences.zh: 秦二年，廣陵人召平為陳勝徇廣陵，未下。

translations.literal: In Qin's second year, Zhao Ping of Guangling campaigned in Guangling for Chen Sheng, but had not taken it.
translations.idiomatic: In Qin's second year, Zhao Ping of Guangling campaigned there for Chen Sheng but had not yet captured it.

sentences.zh: 聞陳勝敗走，秦將章邯且至，乃渡江矯陳王令，拜梁為楚上柱國，曰：「江東已定，急引兵西擊秦。」

translations.literal: Hearing Chen Sheng was defeated and fled, and Qin general Zhang Han was about to arrive, he crossed the river and forged King Chen's order, appointing Liang as Chu's Supreme Pillar of State, saying: "Jiangdong is already settled; urgently lead troops west to strike Qin."
translations.idiomatic: When he heard Chen Sheng had fallen and Zhang Han was approaching, Zhao Ping crossed the river and forged an order from King Chen appointing Xiang Liang Supreme Pillar of Chu, urging him: "Jiangdong is secure; move west at once against Qin."

sentences.zh: 梁乃以八千人渡江而西。

translations.literal: Liang then crossed the river westward with eight thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang then crossed west with eight thousand troops.

sentences.zh: 聞陳嬰已下東陽，使使欲與連和俱西。

translations.literal: Hearing Chen Ying had already taken Dongyang, he sent envoys, wishing to join in alliance and go west together.
translations.idiomatic: Hearing Chen Ying had secured Dongyang, Xiang Liang sent envoys to ally with him and march west together.

sentences.zh: 陳嬰者，故東陽令史，居縣，素信，為長者。

translations.literal: Chen Ying was formerly a clerk under the magistrate of Dongyang, living in the county, long trusted, and an elder-minded man.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ying had been a county clerk at Dongyang, widely trusted and regarded as an upright elder.

sentences.zh: 東陽少年殺其令，相聚數千人，欲立長，無適用，乃請陳嬰。

translations.literal: Dongyang youths killed their magistrate and gathered several thousand men; wanting to establish a leader and finding none suitable, they asked Chen Ying.
translations.idiomatic: Dongyang youths killed the magistrate and gathered several thousand men. Wanting a leader and finding none fit, they turned to Chen Ying.

sentences.zh: 嬰謝不能，遂強立之，縣中從之者得二萬人。

translations.literal: Ying declined, saying he could not; they then forcibly established him, and those following him in the county reached twenty thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ying refused, but they forced him into command, and his followers in the county grew to twenty thousand.

sentences.zh: 欲立嬰為王，異軍蒼頭特起。

translations.literal: They wished to establish Ying as king; this separate force in dark headcloths rose distinctly.
translations.idiomatic: They wanted to make Chen Ying king, and this independent Cangtou force rose rapidly.

sentences.zh: 嬰母謂嬰曰：「自吾為乃家婦，聞先故未曾貴。

translations.literal: Ying's mother said to Ying: "Since I entered your family as wife, I have heard your forebears were never noble.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ying's mother said, "Since I married into this house, I have never heard that our family held high rank.

sentences.zh: 今暴得大名，不祥。

translations.literal: Now suddenly gaining great fame is inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: To gain such fame overnight is inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 不如有所屬，事成猶得封侯，事敗易以亡，非世所指名也。」

translations.literal: Better to attach yourself to someone; if affairs succeed you may still be enfeoffed as marquis, if affairs fail it is easier to disappear, and your name will not be what the world points at."
translations.idiomatic: Better to attach yourself to another power. If things succeed, you may still win a marquisate; if they fail, you can more easily vanish and avoid becoming the name everyone condemns."

sentences.zh: 嬰乃不敢為王，謂其軍曰：「項氏世世將家，有名於楚，今欲舉大事，將非其人，不可。

translations.literal: Ying then did not dare become king and said to his army: "The Xiang clan has for generations been a family of generals, renowned in Chu. If we now undertake great affairs, if the leader is not that kind of man, it cannot be done.
translations.idiomatic: So Chen Ying did not dare claim kingship. He told his troops, "The Xiang clan has been famous Chu generals for generations. In a great uprising like this, we must follow the right kind of leader.

sentences.zh: 我倚名族，亡秦必矣。」

translations.literal: If I rely on a famous clan, Qin's destruction will certainly come."
translations.idiomatic: If we rely on such a renowned house, Qin's fall is assured."

sentences.zh: 其眾從之，乃以其兵屬梁。

translations.literal: His men followed him, and he attached his troops to Liang.
translations.idiomatic: His men agreed, and he placed his army under Xiang Liang.

sentences.zh: 梁渡淮，英布、蒲將軍亦以其兵屬焉。

translations.literal: When Liang crossed the Huai, Ying Bu and General Pu also attached their troops to him.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Liang crossed the Huai, Ying Bu and General Pu also joined him with their forces.

sentences.zh: 凡六七萬人，軍下邳。

translations.literal: In all there were sixty to seventy thousand men, and they camped at Xiapi.
translations.idiomatic: In total they had sixty to seventy thousand men and encamped at Xiapi.

sentences.zh: 是時，秦嘉已立景駒為楚王，軍彭城東，欲以距梁。

translations.literal: At this time, Qin Jia had already installed Jing Ju as king of Chu, camped east of Pengcheng, and wished thereby to resist Liang.
translations.idiomatic: By then Qin Jia had set up Jing Ju as Chu king and camped east of Pengcheng to block Xiang Liang.

sentences.zh: 梁謂軍吏曰：「陳王首事，戰不利，未聞所在。

translations.literal: Liang said to his officers: "King Chen first raised the cause. He fared poorly in battle, and his whereabouts are unknown.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang told his officers, "King Chen was first to rise. He lost in battle, and no one knows where he is.

sentences.zh: 今秦嘉背陳王立景駒，大逆亡道。」

translations.literal: Now Qin Jia has betrayed King Chen and set up Jing Ju, greatly rebellious and without way."
translations.idiomatic: Now Qin Jia has betrayed him by installing Jing Ju. That is great treason and utter lawlessness."

sentences.zh: 乃引兵擊秦嘉。

translations.literal: He then led troops to attack Qin Jia.
translations.idiomatic: He therefore led troops against Qin Jia.

sentences.zh: 軍敗走，追至胡陵。

translations.literal: Qin Jia's army was defeated and fled; Liang pursued to Huling.
translations.idiomatic: Qin Jia's forces broke and fled, and Xiang Liang pursued them to Huling.

sentences.zh: 嘉還戰一日，嘉死，軍降。

translations.literal: Jia turned and fought for one day; Jia died, and his army surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Qin Jia turned and fought for a day, then died; his army surrendered.

sentences.zh: 景駒走死梁地。

translations.literal: Jing Ju fled and died in Liang territory.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ju fled and was killed in Liang territory.

sentences.zh: 梁已并秦嘉軍，胡陵，將引而西。

translations.literal: After Liang had absorbed Qin Jia's army at Huling, he was about to lead it west.
translations.idiomatic: After absorbing Qin Jia's troops at Huling, Xiang Liang prepared to march west.

sentences.zh: 章邯至栗，梁使別將朱雞石、餘樊君與戰。

translations.literal: When Zhang Han arrived at Li, Liang sent separate generals Zhu Jishi and Lord Yufan to fight him.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Han reached Li, Xiang Liang sent Zhu Jishi and Lord Yufan as detached commanders to engage him.

sentences.zh: 餘樊君死。

translations.literal: Lord Yufan died.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Yufan was killed.

sentences.zh: 朱雞石敗，亡走胡陵。

translations.literal: Zhu Jishi was defeated and fled to Huling.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Jishi was defeated and fled back to Huling.

sentences.zh: 梁乃引兵入薛，誅朱雞石。

translations.literal: Liang then led troops into Xue and executed Zhu Jishi.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang then marched into Xue and executed Zhu Jishi.

sentences.zh: 梁前使羽別攻襄城，襄城堅守不下。

translations.literal: Earlier Liang had sent Yu separately to attack Xiangcheng; Xiangcheng held firm and would not fall.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, Xiang Liang had sent Xiang Yu to attack Xiangcheng, but the city held out stubbornly.

sentences.zh: 已拔，皆阬之，還報梁。

translations.literal: After it was taken, all were buried alive; Yu returned and reported to Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Once Xiangcheng finally fell, Xiang Yu buried all defenders alive and returned to report.

sentences.zh: 聞陳王定死，召諸別將會薛計事。

translations.literal: Hearing King Chen was certainly dead, Liang summoned various detached generals to meet at Xue and discuss matters.
translations.idiomatic: When news confirmed King Chen's death, Xiang Liang called his detached commanders to Xue for deliberation.

sentences.zh: 時沛公亦從沛往。

translations.literal: At that time the Duke of Pei also came from Pei.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the Duke of Pei also arrived from Pei.

sentences.zh: 居鄛人范增年七十，素好奇計，往說梁曰：「陳勝敗固當。

translations.literal: Fan Zeng of Juchao, aged seventy, long fond of extraordinary strategy, went to persuade Liang, saying: "Chen Sheng's defeat was bound to happen.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Zeng of Juchao, seventy years old and known for bold strategy, came to advise Xiang Liang: "Chen Sheng's defeat was inevitable.

sentences.zh: 夫秦滅六國，楚最亡罪，自懷王入秦不反，楚人憐之至今，故南公稱曰『楚雖三戶，亡秦必楚』。

translations.literal: When Qin destroyed the six states, Chu was least guilty of destruction. Since King Huai entered Qin and did not return, the Chu people have pitied him to this day; therefore Lord Nan said, 'Though Chu has but three households, the one to destroy Qin will surely be Chu.'
translations.idiomatic: Of the six states Qin destroyed, Chu had least cause to perish. Since King Huai entered Qin and never returned, Chu people have mourned him ever since. Thus Lord Nan said, 'Even if Chu is reduced to three households, Qin will still be destroyed by Chu.'

sentences.zh: 今陳勝首事，不立楚後，其勢不長。

translations.literal: Now Chen Sheng first raised the cause, but did not establish a Chu heir; his momentum could not last.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Sheng rose first, but because he did not install a rightful Chu heir, his position could not endure.

sentences.zh: 今君起江東，楚蠭起之將皆爭附君者，以君世世楚將，為能復立楚之後也。」

translations.literal: Now you rise in Jiangdong; all Chu commanders who have risen like swarms compete to attach themselves to you because your family has for generations been Chu generals and can restore Chu's line."
translations.idiomatic: Now you have risen in Jiangdong. Chu forces are swarming to join you because your house has served as Chu generals for generations, and they believe you can restore Chu's royal line."

sentences.zh: 於是梁乃求楚懷王孫心，在民間為人牧羊，立以為楚懷王，從民望也。

translations.literal: Thereupon Liang sought Xin, grandson of King Huai of Chu, who was among the people herding sheep, and set him up as King Huai of Chu, following popular hopes.
translations.idiomatic: So Xiang Liang searched out Xin, grandson of King Huai, then a common shepherd, and enthroned him as King Huai to match popular sentiment.

sentences.zh: 陳嬰為上柱國，封五縣。

translations.literal: Chen Ying became Supreme Pillar of State and was enfeoffed with five counties.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ying was made Supreme Pillar of State and granted five counties.

sentences.zh: 與懷王都盱台。

translations.literal: Together with King Huai he made his capital at Xutai.
translations.idiomatic: He and King Huai established their seat at Xutai.

sentences.zh: 梁自號武信君，引兵攻亢父。

translations.literal: Liang styled himself Lord Wuxin and led troops to attack Kangfu.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang took the title Lord Wuxin and led troops to attack Kangfu.

sentences.zh: 初，章邯既殺齊王田儋於臨菑，田假復自立為齊王。

translations.literal: At first, after Zhang Han had killed Qi king Tian Dan at Linzi, Tian Jia again set himself up as king of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, after Zhang Han killed King Tian Dan of Qi at Linzi, Tian Jia once more declared himself king of Qi.

sentences.zh: 儋弟榮走保東阿，章邯追圍之。

translations.literal: Dan's younger brother Rong fled and held Dong'e; Zhang Han pursued and besieged him.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Dan's younger brother Tian Rong fled to Dong'e and held it, but Zhang Han pursued and besieged him.

sentences.zh: 梁引兵救東阿，大破秦軍東阿，田榮即引兵歸，逐王假。

translations.literal: Liang led troops to rescue Dong'e, greatly defeated Qin forces there, and Tian Rong then led troops back and drove out king Jia.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang marched to relieve Dong'e, badly defeated Qin there, and Tian Rong then returned with troops and expelled King Jia.

sentences.zh: 假亡走楚，相田角亡走趙。

translations.literal: Jia fled to Chu; chancellor Tian Jiao fled to Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Jia fled to Chu, while Chancellor Tian Jiao fled to Zhao.

sentences.zh: 角弟閒，故將，居趙不敢歸。

translations.literal: Jiao's younger brother Xian, formerly a general, stayed in Zhao and did not dare return.
translations.idiomatic: Jiao's younger brother Tian Xian, a former general, remained in Zhao and dared not return.

sentences.zh: 田榮立儋子市為齊王。

translations.literal: Tian Rong established Dan's son Shi as king of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Rong installed Tian Dan's son Shi as king of Qi.

sentences.zh: 梁已破東阿下軍，遂追秦軍。

translations.literal: After Liang had broken the forces below Dong'e, he then pursued the Qin army.
translations.idiomatic: After defeating Qin's lower camp at Dong'e, Xiang Liang pressed the pursuit.

sentences.zh: 數使使趣齊兵俱西。

translations.literal: He repeatedly sent envoys urging Qi troops to go west together.
translations.idiomatic: He repeatedly sent envoys to urge Qi to send troops west in concert.

sentences.zh: 榮曰：「楚殺田假，趙殺田角、田閒，乃發兵。」

translations.literal: Rong said: "If Chu kills Tian Jia and Zhao kills Tian Jiao and Tian Xian, then I will dispatch troops."
translations.idiomatic: Tian Rong said, "Only if Chu kills Tian Jia and Zhao kills Tian Jiao and Tian Xian will I send troops."

sentences.zh: 梁曰：「田假與國之王，窮來歸我，不忍殺。」

translations.literal: Liang said: "Tian Jia was king of an allied state; when pressed he came to me for refuge, and I cannot bear to kill him."
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang said, "Tian Jia is a king of another state. He came to me in distress, and I cannot bring myself to kill him."

sentences.zh: 趙亦不殺角、閒以市於齊。

translations.literal: Zhao also did not kill Tian Jiao and Tian Xian, keeping them as leverage with Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao likewise spared Tian Jiao and Tian Xian, hoping to bargain with Qi.

sentences.zh: 齊遂不肯發兵助楚。

translations.literal: Qi ultimately refused to send troops to help Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Qi ultimately refused to send troops to aid Chu.

sentences.zh: 梁使羽與沛公別攻城陽，屠之。

translations.literal: Liang sent Yu and the Duke of Pei separately to attack Chengyang and massacre it.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang sent Xiang Yu and the Duke of Pei to attack Chengyang; they slaughtered the city.

sentences.zh: 西破秦軍濮陽東，秦兵收入濮陽。

translations.literal: They defeated the Qin army east of Puyang, and the Qin troops withdrew into Puyang.
translations.idiomatic: They defeated the Qin army east of Puyang, forcing the Qin troops to withdraw into the city.

sentences.zh: 沛公、羽攻定陶。

translations.literal: The Duke of Pei and Yu attacked Dingtao.
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Pei and Xiang Yu then attacked Dingtao.

sentences.zh: 定陶未下，去，西略地至雍丘，大破秦軍，斬李由。

translations.literal: Dingtao did not fall; they left and swept westward to Yongqiu, greatly defeating Qin forces and beheading Li You.
translations.idiomatic: When Dingtao held out, they moved west, ravaged territory to Yongqiu, crushed a Qin army, and killed Li You.

sentences.zh: 還攻外黃，外黃未下。

translations.literal: They returned and attacked Waihuang, but Waihuang did not fall.
translations.idiomatic: They turned back and attacked Waihuang, but the city held out.

sentences.zh: 梁起東阿，比至定陶，再破秦軍，羽等又斬李由，益輕秦，有驕色。

translations.literal: Liang rose from Dong'e; by the time he reached Dingtao he had twice defeated Qin forces, and Yu and others had again beheaded Li You, so they increasingly slighted Qin and showed arrogance.
translations.idiomatic: Marching from Dong'e to Dingtao, Xiang Liang's side had beaten Qin twice and Xiang Yu had killed Li You, and this success made them increasingly contemptuous and proud.

sentences.zh: 宋義諫曰：「戰勝而將驕卒惰者敗。

translations.literal: Song Yi remonstrated: "Those who win battles and then grow proud as commanders and lax as soldiers are defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Song Yi warned, "Armies that win and then grow arrogant and slack are bound to lose.

sentences.zh: 今少惰矣，秦兵日益，臣為君畏之。」

translations.literal: Now [we] are somewhat lax, while Qin troops increase daily; for my lord I fear this."
translations.idiomatic: We are already growing lax, while Qin strength grows every day. I fear this for you."

sentences.zh: 梁不聽。

translations.literal: Liang did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang did not listen.

sentences.zh: 乃使宋義於齊。

translations.literal: He then sent Song Yi to Qi.
translations.idiomatic: He then dispatched Song Yi to Qi.

sentences.zh: 道遇齊使者高陵君顯，曰：「公將見武信君乎？」

translations.literal: On the way, he encountered the Qi envoy Gao Ling Jun Xian, who said: 'Are you going to see Lord Wu Xin?'
translations.idiomatic: Along the way, he encountered the Qi envoy Lord Gao Ling Xian, who asked: 'Are you going to see Lord Wu Xin?'

translations.literal: Song Yi said: 'Yes.'
translations.idiomatic: Song Yi replied: 'Yes.'

sentences.zh: 義曰：「臣論武信君軍必敗。

translations.literal: Yi said: "I judge Lord Wuxin's army will certainly be defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Song Yi said, "By my judgment, Lord Wuxin's army will surely be defeated.

sentences.zh: 公徐行則免，疾行則及禍。」

translations.literal: If you travel slowly you may avoid disaster; if you hurry you will meet calamity."
translations.idiomatic: If you proceed slowly you may escape; if you rush, disaster will catch you."

sentences.zh: 秦果悉起兵益章邯，夜銜枚擊楚，大破之定陶，梁死。

translations.literal: Qin indeed fully raised troops to reinforce Zhang Han, and at night with gagged bits attacked Chu, greatly defeating it at Dingtao; Liang died.
translations.idiomatic: Qin did reinforce Zhang Han with full strength, then launched a silent night attack and crushed Chu at Dingtao; Xiang Liang was killed.

sentences.zh: 沛公與羽去外黃，攻陳留，陳留堅守不下。

translations.literal: The Duke of Pei and Yu left Waihuang and attacked Chenliu; Chenliu held firm and did not fall.
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Pei and Xiang Yu left Waihuang and attacked Chenliu, but the city held fast.

sentences.zh: 沛公、羽相與謀曰：「今梁軍敗，士卒恐。」

translations.literal: The Duke of Pei and Yu consulted each other, saying: "Now Liang's army is defeated, and the soldiers are afraid."
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Pei and Xiang Yu conferred: "Xiang Liang's army is beaten, and the troops are frightened."

sentences.zh: 乃與呂臣俱引兵而東。

translations.literal: So together with Lü Chen they led troops eastward.
translations.idiomatic: So they joined Lu Chen and withdrew east with their forces.

sentences.zh: 呂臣軍彭城東，羽軍彭城西，沛公軍碭。

translations.literal: Lü Chen camped east of Pengcheng, Yu camped west of Pengcheng, and the Duke of Pei camped at Dang.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Chen camped east of Pengcheng, Xiang Yu west of it, and the Duke of Pei at Dang.

sentences.zh: 章邯已破梁軍，則以為楚地兵不足憂，乃渡河北擊趙，大破之。

translations.literal: After Zhang Han had broken Liang's army, he considered Chu's local forces not worth worrying about, then crossed north of the Yellow River to attack Zhao and greatly defeated it.
translations.idiomatic: After defeating Xiang Liang, Zhang Han judged Chu no longer dangerous, crossed north of the Yellow River, and dealt Zhao a heavy defeat.

sentences.zh: 當此之時，趙歇為王，陳餘為將，張耳為相，走入鉅鹿城。

translations.literal: At that time Zhao Xie was king, Chen Yu was general, and Zhang Er was chancellor; they fled into Julu city.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Zhao Xie was king, with Chen Yu as general and Zhang Er as chancellor; they retreated into Julu.

sentences.zh: 秦將王離、涉閒圍鉅鹿，章邯軍其南，築甬道而輸之粟。

translations.literal: Qin generals Wang Li and She Xian besieged Julu, while Zhang Han camped to its south, building supply corridors to transport grain.
translations.idiomatic: Qin generals Wang Li and She Xian besieged Julu, while Zhang Han camped south of it and built protected supply lines for grain.

sentences.zh: 陳餘將卒數萬人軍鉅鹿北，所謂河北軍也。

translations.literal: Chen Yu led tens of thousands of troops and camped north of Julu, the force called the Hebei army.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu commanded tens of thousands north of Julu; this was the so-called Hebei army.

sentences.zh: 宋義所遇齊使者高陵君顯見楚懷王曰：「宋義論武信君必敗，數日果敗。

translations.literal: The envoy of Qi whom Song Yi encountered, Lord Gaoling Xian, saw King Huai of Chu and said: "Song Yi predicted Lord Wuxin would be defeated, and after several days he indeed was.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Gaoling Xian, the Qi envoy Song Yi met, told King Huai: "Song Yi predicted Lord Wuxin's defeat, and days later it happened.

sentences.zh: 軍未戰先見敗徵，可謂知兵矣。」

translations.literal: Before armies fought he first saw signs of defeat; this can be called knowing warfare."
translations.idiomatic: He foresaw defeat before battle began. That is true military insight."

sentences.zh: 王召宋義與計事而說之，因以為上將軍；

translations.literal: The king summoned Song Yi to discuss affairs and approved him, therefore made him supreme general.
translations.idiomatic: The king summoned Song Yi, liked his counsel, and appointed him supreme commander.

sentences.zh: 羽為魯公，為次將，范增為末將。

translations.literal: Yu was made Duke of Lu and second general; Fan Zeng was last general.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu was made Duke of Lu and deputy general, with Fan Zeng as rear general.

sentences.zh: 諸別將皆屬，號卿子冠軍。

translations.literal: All detached generals belonged to him, and he was titled Qingzi Champion.
translations.idiomatic: All other commanders were placed under him, and he was styled Qingzi Guanjun.

sentences.zh: 北救趙，至安陽，留不進。

translations.literal: He went north to rescue Zhao, reached Anyang, and stayed without advancing.
translations.idiomatic: He marched north to aid Zhao, reached Anyang, and then halted.

sentences.zh: 秦三年，羽謂宋義曰：「今秦軍圍鉅鹿，疾引兵渡河，楚擊其外，趙應其內，破秦軍必矣。」

translations.literal: In Qin's third year, Yu said to Song Yi: "Now Qin surrounds Julu. Quickly lead troops across the river; Chu strikes from without and Zhao responds from within, and we will surely break Qin."
translations.idiomatic: In Qin's third year Xiang Yu told Song Yi, "Qin has Julu encircled. Cross the river at once: we attack from outside and Zhao from within, and Qin will be broken."

sentences.zh: 宋義曰：「不然。

translations.literal: Song Yi said: 'Not so.
translations.idiomatic: Song Yi replied: 'Not so.'

sentences.zh: 夫搏牛之虻不可以破蝨。

translations.literal: A gadfly that bites cattle cannot break a louse.
translations.idiomatic: A gadfly that troubles oxen cannot crush a louse.

sentences.zh: 今秦攻趙，戰勝則兵罷，我承其敝；

translations.literal: Now Qin attacks Zhao; if they win, their troops will be exhausted, and we can take advantage of their fatigue;
translations.idiomatic: Now Qin attacks Zhao. If they win, their troops will be worn out, and we can exploit their exhaustion.

sentences.zh: 不勝，則我引兵鼓行而西，必舉秦矣。

translations.literal: If they do not win, then we can lead our troops and march west beating drums, and we will certainly take Qin.
translations.idiomatic: If they lose, we can lead our troops westward in a triumphal march and will surely conquer Qin.

sentences.zh: 故不如先鬥秦、趙。

translations.literal: Therefore it is better to first let Qin and Zhao fight.
translations.idiomatic: So it is better to let Qin and Zhao wear each other down first.

sentences.zh: 夫擊輕銳，我不如公；

translations.literal: In charging with light elite troops, I am not equal to you;
translations.idiomatic: In leading shock troops, I am not your equal;

sentences.zh: 坐運籌策，公不如我。」

translations.literal: In sitting and working strategy, you are not equal to me."
translations.idiomatic: But in seated planning and calculation, you are not my equal."

sentences.zh: 因下令軍中曰：「猛如虎，佷如羊，貪如狼，強不可令者，皆斬。」

translations.literal: Thereupon he issued orders in the army saying: "Fierce as tigers, stubborn as sheep, greedy as wolves, and those too strong to be commanded, all shall be beheaded."
translations.idiomatic: He then ordered the army: "Men fierce like tigers, obstinate like sheep, greedy like wolves, or unwilling to obey command will all be executed."

sentences.zh: 遣其子襄相齊，身送之無鹽，飲酒高會。

translations.literal: He sent his son Xiang as chancellor to Qi and personally escorted him to Wuyan, holding a grand drinking feast.
translations.idiomatic: He sent his son Xiang to serve in Qi and personally escorted him to Wuyan, where he held lavish drinking banquets.

sentences.zh: 天寒大雨，士卒凍飢。

translations.literal: The weather was bitterly cold with heavy rain, and the soldiers were freezing and hungry.
translations.idiomatic: Rain and cold intensified, and the troops were freezing and starving.

sentences.zh: 羽曰：「將戮力而攻秦，久留不行。

translations.literal: Yu said: "We are to join strength and attack Qin, yet [he] stays long and does not move.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu said, "We are supposed to unite and strike Qin, yet he lingers and will not move.

sentences.zh: 今歲飢民貧，卒食半菽，軍無見糧，乃飲酒高會，不引兵渡河因趙食，與并力擊秦，乃曰『承其敝』。

translations.literal: Now in this year there is famine and the people are poor; soldiers eat only half-rations of beans, the army has no visible stores, yet he drinks at grand banquets and does not lead troops across the river to use Zhao's grain and strike Qin together, but says 'we will take advantage of their exhaustion.'
translations.idiomatic: This year is famine, the people are impoverished, soldiers survive on half-rations, and the army has no reserves; yet he drinks at high feasts instead of crossing the river, using Zhao's supplies, and joining force against Qin, all while saying we should 'wait to profit from exhaustion.'

sentences.zh: 夫以秦之強，攻新造之趙，其勢必舉趙。

translations.literal: Now with Qin's strength attacking newly formed Zhao, the trend will surely be to raise Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: With Qin this strong attacking a newly restored Zhao, Zhao will surely be crushed.

sentences.zh: 趙舉秦強，何敝之承！

translations.literal: If Zhao is taken and Qin grows stronger, what exhaustion can we exploit?
translations.idiomatic: If Zhao falls and Qin becomes stronger, what 'exhaustion' will be left for us to exploit?

sentences.zh: 且國兵新破，王坐不安席，掃境內而屬將軍，國家安危，在此一舉。

translations.literal: Moreover, our state's army has just been broken, the king cannot sit securely, and he has swept the whole realm to entrust it to the general. The safety or peril of the state lies in this single action.
translations.idiomatic: Our army was only just defeated, the king cannot rest easy, and he has committed the whole realm to this command. The state's survival depends on this one move.

sentences.zh: 今不卹士卒而徇私宴，非社稷之臣也。」

translations.literal: Now not caring for the soldiers and pursuing private feasts is not the conduct of a minister of the altars of state."
translations.idiomatic: To neglect the troops and indulge private banquets is no service to the state."

sentences.zh: 羽晨朝上將軍宋義，即其帳中斬義頭。

translations.literal: At dawn Yu went to attend Supreme General Song Yi and in his tent cut off Yi's head.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn Xiang Yu entered Song Yi's tent under pretext of audience and beheaded him.

sentences.zh: 出令軍中曰：「宋義與齊謀反楚，楚王陰令籍誅之。」

translations.literal: He came out and ordered the army: "Song Yi conspired with Qi to betray Chu; King Chu secretly ordered Ji to execute him."
translations.idiomatic: Coming out, he announced to the army: "Song Yi plotted with Qi against Chu; the king secretly ordered me to execute him."

sentences.zh: 諸將讋服，莫敢枝梧。

translations.literal: All generals were cowed and submitted; none dared resist.
translations.idiomatic: All commanders were terrified into submission, and none dared object.

sentences.zh: 皆曰：「首立楚者，將軍家也。

translations.literal: All said: 'The first to establish Chu was the general's family.
translations.idiomatic: They all declared: 'Your family was the first to restore the Chu royal line.'

sentences.zh: 今將軍誅亂。」

translations.literal: Now the general has executed the rebel.'
translations.idiomatic: Now you, General, have put down the traitor.'

sentences.zh: 乃相與共立羽為假上將軍。

translations.literal: They then together established Yu as Acting Supreme General.
translations.idiomatic: They then jointly appointed Xiang Yu as Acting Supreme Commander.

sentences.zh: 使人追宋義子，及之齊，殺之。

translations.literal: He sent people to pursue Song Yi's son, caught up with him in Qi, and killed him.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched men to pursue Song Yi's son. They caught up with him in Qi and executed him.

sentences.zh: 使桓楚報命於王。

translations.literal: He sent Huan Chu to report this order to the king.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Huan Chu to report events to the king.

sentences.zh: 王因使使立羽為上將軍。

translations.literal: The king then sent envoys to establish Yu as supreme general.
translations.idiomatic: The king then dispatched envoys and formally installed Xiang Yu as supreme commander.

sentences.zh: 羽已殺卿子冠軍，威震楚國，名聞諸侯。

translations.literal: After Yu had killed Qingzi Guanjun, his power shook Chu and his name was heard among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu killed Qingzi Guanjun, his authority shook Chu and his fame spread among the lords.

sentences.zh: 乃遣當陽君、蒲將軍將卒二萬人渡河救鉅鹿。

translations.literal: He then sent Lord Dangyang and General Pu to lead twenty thousand troops across the river to rescue Julu.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent Lord Dangyang and General Pu with twenty thousand men across the river to relieve Julu.

sentences.zh: 戰少利，陳餘復請兵。

translations.literal: The battle gained little advantage, Chen Yu again requested troops.
translations.idiomatic: The fighting yielded scant success, so Chen Yu requested reinforcements once more.

sentences.zh: 羽乃悉引兵渡河。

translations.literal: Yu then led all troops across the river.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu then brought his full force across the river.

sentences.zh: 已渡，皆湛舡，破釜甑，燒廬舍，持三日糧，視士必死，無還心。

translations.literal: After crossing, all sank their boats, broke cauldrons and steamers, burned shelters, and carried only three days' grain, showing the soldiers they must die and had no thought of return.
translations.idiomatic: After crossing, they sank their boats, smashed cooking gear, burned camps, and carried only three days of food, forcing every soldier to fight to the death with no thought of retreat.

sentences.zh: 於是至則圍王離，與秦軍遇，九戰，絕甬道，大破之，殺蘇角，虜王離。

translations.literal: Thereupon when they arrived they besieged Wang Li. Meeting Qin troops, they fought nine battles, cut the supply corridor, greatly defeated them, killed Su Jiao, and captured Wang Li.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival they enclosed Wang Li, fought nine engagements, severed Qin's supply lines, won a crushing victory, killed Su Jiao, and captured Wang Li.

sentences.zh: 涉閒不降，自燒殺。

translations.literal: She Xian would not surrender and burned himself to death.
translations.idiomatic: She Xian refused surrender and burned himself to death.

sentences.zh: 當是時，楚兵冠諸侯。

translations.literal: At this time, Chu troops were the best among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: At this juncture, the Chu army was preeminent among all the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 諸侯軍救鉅鹿者十餘壁，莫敢縱兵。

translations.literal: The lords' armies that came to rescue Julu had more than ten camps, yet none dared send out troops.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten allied camps had come to relieve Julu, but none dared commit troops.

sentences.zh: 及楚擊秦，諸侯皆從壁上觀。

translations.literal: When Chu attacked Qin, all the lords watched from atop their ramparts.
translations.idiomatic: When Chu finally attacked, the allied armies only watched from their walls.

sentences.zh: 楚戰士無不一當十，呼聲動天地。

translations.literal: Among Chu's warriors there was none who was not a match for ten; their battle cries shook Heaven and Earth.
translations.idiomatic: Every Chu fighter fought like ten men, and their shouts shook heaven and earth.

sentences.zh: 諸侯軍人人惴恐。

translations.literal: The lords' armies were all trembling with fear.
translations.idiomatic: The allied armies were all terrified.

sentences.zh: 於是楚已破秦軍，羽見諸侯將，入轅門，膝行而前，莫敢仰視。

translations.literal: Thus after Chu had broken Qin forces, when Yu met the lords' generals and entered the camp gate, they advanced on their knees, and none dared raise his eyes.
translations.idiomatic: After Chu's victory, when Xiang Yu received the other generals at the camp gate, they crawled forward on their knees, not daring to look up.

sentences.zh: 羽繇是始為諸侯上將軍，兵皆屬焉。

translations.literal: From this point Yu began to be supreme general over the lords, and their troops all belonged to him.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, Xiang Yu was acknowledged as overlord commander among the lords, and their armies fell under his authority.

sentences.zh: 章邯軍棘原，羽軍漳南，相持未戰。

translations.literal: Zhang Han camped at Jiyuan and Yu camped south of the Zhang River; they faced each other without battle.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han camped at Jiyuan while Xiang Yu camped south of the Zhang; the two armies faced off without fighting.

sentences.zh: 秦軍數卻，二世使人讓章邯。

translations.literal: The Qin army repeatedly retreated, the Second Emperor sent people to rebuke Zhang Han.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin army suffered repeated setbacks. The Second Emperor sent envoys to reprimand Zhang Han.

sentences.zh: 章邯恐，使長史欣請事。

translations.literal: Zhang Han was afraid, sent his chief clerk Xin to request surrender terms.
translations.idiomatic: Terrified, Zhang Han sent his chief clerk Sima Xin to negotiate surrender terms.

sentences.zh: 至咸陽，留司馬門三日，趙高不見，有不信之心。

translations.literal: They arrived at Xianyang, waited at the Sima Gate for three days, Zhao Gao did not see them, had distrustful thoughts.
translations.idiomatic: They reached Xianyang and waited at the Sima Gate for three days. Zhao Gao refused to meet them, harboring suspicions.

sentences.zh: 長史欣恐，還走，不敢出故道。

translations.literal: The chief clerk Xin was afraid; he fled back and dared not take the old route.
translations.idiomatic: Chief Clerk Xin was afraid and fled back by a different route.

sentences.zh: 趙高果使人追之，不及。

translations.literal: Zhao Gao indeed sent men to pursue him, but they did not catch him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Gao did send pursuers after him, but they failed to catch him.

sentences.zh: 欣至軍，報曰：「事亡可為者。

translations.literal: When Xin reached the army, he reported: "There is nothing to be done.
translations.idiomatic: When Xin returned to camp, he said, "There is no saving this situation.

sentences.zh: 相國趙高顓國主斷。

translations.literal: Chancellor Zhao Gao monopolizes state power and personally decides everything.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Zhao Gao has monopolized power and decides everything himself.

sentences.zh: 今戰而勝，高嫉吾功；

translations.literal: If we fight and win, Zhao Gao will envy my merit;
translations.idiomatic: If we win, Zhao Gao will resent our merit.

sentences.zh: 不勝，不免於死。

translations.literal: If we do not win, we cannot escape death.
translations.idiomatic: If we lose, we die all the same.

sentences.zh: 願將軍熟計之。」

translations.literal: I beg the general to think deeply on this."
translations.idiomatic: General, you must weigh this carefully."

sentences.zh: 陳餘亦遺章邯書曰：「白起為秦將，南并鄢郢，北阬馬服，攻城略地，不可勝計，而卒賜死。

translations.literal: Chen Yu also sent Zhang Han a letter, saying: "Bai Qi was Qin general; south he annexed Yan and Ying, north he buried the men of Mafou alive, attacking cities and seizing lands beyond count, yet in the end he was ordered to die.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu also wrote to Zhang Han: "Bai Qi was a Qin general who conquered endlessly, yet was ultimately forced to commit suicide.

sentences.zh: 蒙恬為秦將，北逐戎人，開榆中地數十里，竟斬陽周。

translations.literal: Meng Tian was Qin general; north he drove off the Rong and opened dozens of li in Yuzhong, yet in the end was beheaded at Yangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Meng Tian drove back northern tribes and opened vast frontier lands, yet he too was executed.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why was that?

sentences.zh: 功多，秦不能封，因以法誅之。

translations.literal: With much merit, Qin could not reward them, but instead executed them by law.
translations.idiomatic: Qin did not reward great merit; it used legal charges to kill its own great commanders.

sentences.zh: 今將軍為秦將三歲矣，所亡失已十萬數，而諸侯並起茲益多。

translations.literal: Now, General, you have been Qin's commander for three years; your losses already number over one hundred thousand, while the lords rising against Qin grow ever more numerous.
translations.idiomatic: You have commanded Qin armies for three years, lost over one hundred thousand men, and meanwhile the anti-Qin lords only keep multiplying.

sentences.zh: 彼趙高素諛日久，今事急，亦恐二世誅之，故欲以法誅將軍以塞責，使人更代以脫其禍。

translations.literal: That Zhao Gao has long flattered [the court]; now with affairs urgent, he too fears the Second Emperor will execute him, so he wishes to execute the general by law to shift blame, and replace you with another to free himself from disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Gao has lived by flattery for years. In this crisis he fears punishment from the Second Emperor, so he means to sacrifice you under legal pretext, replace you, and save himself.

sentences.zh: 將軍居外久，多內隙，有功亦誅，亡功亦誅。

translations.literal: General, you have long been outside; there are many internal rifts. With merit you are executed; without merit you are also executed.
translations.idiomatic: You have been away from court too long and are now politically isolated. With victories you may be killed, and without victories you will also be killed.

sentences.zh: 且天之亡秦，無愚智皆知之。

translations.literal: Moreover, Heaven's abandonment of Qin, both wise and foolish all know it.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, Heaven has clearly abandoned Qin. Even the foolish know this.

sentences.zh: 今將軍內不能直諫，外為亡國將，孤立而欲長存，豈不哀哉！

translations.literal: Now, General, inwardly you cannot remonstrate directly, outwardly you serve as general of a doomed state; isolated yet wishing long survival, is this not pitiable!
translations.idiomatic: Now you cannot speak truth at court, and in the field you command for a dying dynasty. Alone and hoping to survive, is this not tragic?

sentences.zh: 將軍何不還兵與諸侯為從，南面稱孤，孰與身伏斧質，妻子為戮乎？」

translations.literal: Why does the general not turn troops back and form alliance with the lords, face south and call yourself ruler? How does this compare with submitting your body to axe and block while wife and children are executed?"
translations.idiomatic: Why not turn your army and join the lords, rule in your own right, and live? Is that not better than kneeling for execution while your family is slaughtered?"

sentences.zh: 章邯狐疑，陰使候始成使羽，欲約。

translations.literal: Zhang Han was uncertain and secretly sent his marquis Shicheng as envoy to Yu, wishing to make an agreement.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han hesitated and secretly sent his aide Shicheng to Xiang Yu to negotiate terms.

sentences.zh: 約未成，羽使蒲將軍引兵渡三戶，軍漳南，與秦戰，再破之。

translations.literal: Before terms were settled, Yu sent General Pu to lead troops across Sanhu, camp south of the Zhang, and fight Qin, defeating them twice.
translations.idiomatic: Before an agreement was reached, Xiang Yu sent General Pu across Sanhu; they camped south of the Zhang and beat Qin twice.

sentences.zh: 羽悉引兵擊秦軍汙水上，大破之。

translations.literal: Yu then led all troops to attack Qin at the Wu River and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu then personally led the full army against Qin at the Wu River and won a major victory.
